title: what a feeling to be right here
summary: John and Jordan’s Valentines morning starts with a lot of glitter and ends with a lot of sex.
body:
Jordan wakes up to glitter on the pillow. 

It’s in his eyelashes, in the corner of his mouth, smeared over his cheekbone. His first thought is to panic, thinking one of the lads has played some horrific prank and dyed his face on a night out.

But then he hears humming. Soft, tuneless humming from somewhere near the end of the bed. Then it hits him – home. He’s home, at his and John’s bedroom, and there’s no hangover pounding behind his eyes. Just this… weird glitter.

He squints blearily down the bed.

John’s on his knees at the foot of it, wearing one of Jordan’s old Everton training tops that hangs loose off one shoulder but is somehow too short on him, and the tiniest pair of Valentine’s boxers known to mankind. Pink, with little hearts and arrows. His hair’s a total mess, sticking up all over, and he’s currently wrapping the footboard with fairy lights.

“What,” John says hoarsely, “in the name of fuck have you done to my bed, pet?”

John startles, nearly drops the fairy lights, then beams up at him like the sun’s come out. 

“You’re awake!” He clambers up onto the mattress, and crawls towards Jordan on all fours. “Don’t freak out, yeah, just stay there a sec.”

“I’d love to stay here,” Jordan mutters, dragging a hand down his face. Glitter transfers to his fingers. “If I can find my eyes under all this bloody–whatever this is.”

John snorts and reaches out, swiping at Jordan’s cheek. “Sorry. Might’ve got a bit enthusiastic with the confetti.”

Jordan blinks properly, lets his vision focus, and finally gets the full picture. 

There are paper hearts stuck to the headboard. Fairy lights, all around the foot of the bed. A little vase with three supermarket roses on the bedside table, and a tray on the floor with what looks like burnt toast, orange juice, and something that might have been an egg.

“Happy Valentine’s,” John says, suddenly shy. “Didn’t wake you, did I?”

Jordan takes a look at him. His cheeks are flushed, glitter on his collarbone. The big lad who crunches into tackles for fun is kneeling on his bed in stupid boxers, waiting for Jordan’s verdict like he’s about to be graded.

“Nah,” Jordan says, his throat going tight. “Didn’t wake me.”

He reaches out, hooks a hand round the back of John’s neck and drags him in. John squeaks, almost knees Jordan in the thigh but then collapses onto him, half laughing. 

“You’re all covered in glue, I swear,” Jordan grumbles into his hair, kissing his temple, then his jaw. “And you burned the toast.”

“Don’t be ungrateful,” John mumbles back, but he’s grinning wide. “There’s loads of jam. It hides the crispy burnt bits.”

He wriggles off Jordan to grab the tray, and Jordan props himself up on his elbows, watching him, that stupid ache in his chest getting worse.

He’s had flashy Valentine’s before, with other people. It’s always fancy restaurants and expensive jewellery, tickets to places he never really wanted to go to. This is… not that. This is glitter stuck in his boyfriend’s beard, and toast hard enough to break a window, and fairy lights all over his bed. 

He loves it more than anything he’s ever loved.

John sets the tray between them carefully. “Here you go. Michelin-star charcoal. Bon apetit.”

Jordan snorts, gives in, and takes a bite. It’s actually not that bad. The jam does do a heroic work at hiding all the burnt parts.”

“Not awful.”

“High praise, coming from you,” John says. “There’s juice. And also, I got you something.”

John hesitates, then reaches under his pillow and pulls out a little red envelope. It’s fat, corners slightly bent from clearly being hidden badly. 

“Johnny, you didn’t have to…”

“I know I don’t,” John interrupts. “I wanted to.”

He shoves it at Jordan, then immediately curls in on himself, arms around his knees, watching with an anxious curiosity.

“Open it, then.”

Jordan wipes his glittery fingers on the duvet (which he will complain about later, then blame John for) and tears the envelop open. A folded card spills out, hand-drawn hearts all over the front. Something heavier thuds onto the sheets. He picks that up first.

It’s a keyring. A tiny acrylic version of the two of them, some fanart – John must’ve tracked the artist down. They’re both in an England kit, John with a tongue between his teeth and Jordan behind him, arms around his waist, kissing the top of his head. Little pink hearts drawn around them. 

“Christ,” he breathes. “You’re such a sap.”

John groans, throwing himself onto his back and covering his face with both hands. “I’m disgusting. I deserve to be locked away.”

Jordan laughs, and flicks the card open. Inside, John’s handwriting is all over the place, lines sloping down because for some reason that boy can’t write in a straight line.

Pickers,

I know we said no big gifts this year because we’re trying to be grown-ups or whatever it is you said. But you’re stuck with me, so here we are.

Thank you for making everywhere feel like home, even when we’re in shitty hotels with thin walls. Thank you for shouting at me when I leave my socks everywhere and then kissing me anyway. I know you can’t stand to be mad at me.

You’re my favourite person in this world.

Happy Valentine’s Day, you big softie. I love you.

John x

P.S. The keyring’s so you will always come home to me

Jordan stares at the words until they blur a little. His throat’s properly closed up now. He swallows the lump, card trembling between his fingers, and drags his gaze up. John’s peaking at him through his fingers, eyes wide with that bare-faced hope that still floors him after all this time. 

“Well?” John says. “It’s not too cringe, is it? I can write you a new one that just says u up? If you want. Granted it won’t be–”

Jordan tosses the card onto the bedside table and launches himself forward, flattening John into the mattress. He lets out a delighted oof from beneath him, arms immediately winding around.

“I love you,” Jordan mutters into his neck, then pulls back enough to kiss him. John tastes like jam and burnt bread. He slots a hand under the back of his shirt, and feels John shiver. 

When they finally break apart, John’s eyes are soft and shiny. “So… you like it?”

“‘Course I do,” Jordan says. “I’m keepin’ that card forever. Gonna laminate it. Put it up at Finch, maybe, so everyone has to read how much you fancy me.”

That earns him a laugh. Jordan drags his mouth down to the line of his jaw and murmurs, “come here, baby. Get those pretty lips on me.”

John’s eyes flutter like he’s just been handed the best present of his life. “N-now?”

“Now,” Jordan says. “All I can think about ever since I woke up covered in bloody glitter.”

John’s already shifting, already sliding down the bed. His palms skim over Jordan’s ribs, his hips, his thighs. There’s a moment where he looks up, kneeling between Jordan’s legs. Something soft flickers across his face, this quiet, adoring thing. 

Then John hooks his fingers under the waistband of Jordan’s boxers, tugging them down. His cock springs up, and John’s pupils blow slightly wide like it’s his favourite sight in the world.

“Fuck,” he mumbles, leaning in. “Missed him.”

Jordan laughs weakly. “You’re talkin’ to it now?” 

“Shh,” John says, brushing his nose along the underside, inhaling like he’s getting drunk on it.

Jordan’s about to make some stupid comeback, but then John licks a slow stripe up the length of him, a long, warm drag of tongue from base to tip, and every coherent thought drops ocean out of Jordan’s head.

“Jesus Christ, sweetheart…”

John hums, pleased, his mouth wrapping around the tip. The heat makes Jordan’s hips jerk, a helpless thrust he tries to bite back. John steadies him, squeezing gently as he takes him deeper. His lips stretch around the thickness, tongue swirling, teasing that tender spot just under the head. And Jordan feels it everywhere, his toes curling hard into the sheets.

He can’t stop touching. One hand in John’s hair, the other cupping his cheek, stroking the soft skin there. “Look at me.”

And John does. Eyes glossy, mouth full, cheeks hollowing as he keeps eye contact and sucks him deeper.

“Christ, that’s it… yeah, that’s it baby, fuck–”

John gags lightly as the tip hits the back of his throat, then does it again deliberately. Drool slips down his chin, dripping onto Jordan’s lower belly.

“Think you’re tryin’ to kill me,” Jordan gets out, voice breaking a little.

John releases him with a wet pop, licking his lips. “Not tryna kill you. I just want to–” he bends, licks up the bead of pre-cum at the tip, eyes fluttering, “–make you lose your mind.”

“Mission accomplished,” Jordan mutters, grabbing him by the waist and hauling him up. John collapses on top of him, rubbing their noses together and Jordan can feel him already, hard, boxers tented, his hips shifting like he can’t stand not being touched.

“Take ‘em off.”

John shivers and nods, wriggling out of his tiny Valentine’s boxers. Jordan runs his hands over his thighs, his hips, the curve of his arse. 

“Get on me,” Jordan instructs while guiding him up, lining them together. Their cocks brush, an electric slide that rips a small whine out of John’s throat.

“Jordan…”

“Right here, pet,” Jordan murmurs. “You’re alright.”

He climbs higher onto Jordan’s hips, but Jordan’s hand catches his waist. “Not yet. Let me get you ready real quick.”

John’s breath stutters. He nods immediately. Jordan’s hands slide down, brushing the crease where John’s thighs rest on his hips. He guides John up just enough so he can reach. John lifts himself obediently, arms braced on Jordan’s chest.

Jordan spits into his fingers, then brings his hand down between John’s legs. The second his fingertips graze him, John’s whole body jerks.

“Easy,” Jordan says, even though he’s already teasing the rim. “You’re clenchin’ already. You were thinking about this since before I woke up, weren’t you?”

“Since– fuck– since before. I’m always thinking about you.”

Jordan presses his forehead to John’s stomach, breathing him in, then pushes the tip of one finger past the tight ring of muscle. He works the finger in gradually, curling it just a little, enough to make John’s thighs tremble around him.

John bites down on his lip, panting. “More,” he whispers. “Jordan, more, please.”

Jordan adds a second finger, sliding in alongside the first. John chokes on a sound, something he would’ve been embarrassed by any other time but not now, not with Jordan whispering praise against his skin, not with those thick fingers stretching him open.

“There you go,” Jordan breathes, thrusting his fingers slowly and feeling John open around him. 

John drops his head back, throat bared, body rocking down onto John’s hand. Jordan scissors his fingers gently, searching, pressing–

John’s entire body jolts.

“Oh, fuck–” he cries, collapsing forward and gripping Jordan’s chest like he’s drowning. “Right there, Jordan, right–”

“I know,” Jordan kisses the underside of his jaw. “I know where.”

“Don’t tease. I need–I need you now, please.”

John sighs as he sinks down slow, inch by inch, warm and tight and perfect, his pretty face scrunching in pleasure, nails digging into Jordan’s chest. “Ohh– god, Jordan, you’re so big.”

Jordan slides a hand up his spine, keeping him steady as John bottoms out with a shaky groan. “Take your time, Stonesy, alright?”

John nods frantically, eyes squeezed shut. He rolls his hips instinctively as he adjusts. Jordan can feel every clench around him, feel John tremble when Jordan thumbs his hipbone gently. John starts moving, little broken sounds escaping him, and Jordan meets him halfway. He’s rough when John wants it, but gentle when he holds him, like John’s some priceless thing he’s terrified of dropping. 

“Ridin’ me like you’re made for it,” Jordan pants when John moves even faster, lifting his hips to fuck him deeper.

“Feels– feels so good, Jords, fuck, don’t stop.”

“Wouldn’t dream of it,” Jordan mutters, arms wrapping around him. “You’re perfect. Every bit of you. Come here, sweetheart, kiss me.”

John does, messy and urgent, whining into him every time Jordan hits that spot inside that makes his whole body jolt. 

“Jord,” he gasps, his that isn’t even a word, just need.

“I’ve got you. You’re so good for me, so fucking good.”

And then Jordan hits that spot again, and John actually sobs, body convulsing around him. The sudden tightness drags Jordan right to the edge. His thrusts stutter, deepen, then turn frantic. “Jesus, John, you’re squeezin’ me– I’m gonna, fuck I’m gonna come.”

“Inside,” John whimpers, eyes blown wide and frantic. “Inside, please.”

Jordan’s control snaps. He groans and sinks up into John as deep as he can go, burying himself fully as he spills hard inside him. His whole body tenses under John’s, shuddering through the release. John’s still moving, these tiny rocking motions that milk every last drop of cum out of him.

Jordan’s hands cradle the back of John’s head. “That’s it, that’s it, take all of it– fuck–”

John collapses on top of him then, panting. He’s sweat-slick, they’re chest to chest, breathing the same uneven breaths. 

Then Jordan’s voice drops, a growl against John’s ear. “Lie back for me.”

John barely has time to blink before Jordan grips his waist and flips him gently onto his back. He lets out a soft, startled exhale, thern another deeper one when Jordan slides between his thighs, kissing up the inside of one, then the other, pushing his legs wider.

“I thought–”

Jordan cuts him off with a slow lick up the length of his cock that makes John’s whole spine arch. “You thought I was done?” He wraps his lips around the head, sucking slow and deep while his hand drifts back down between John’s legs. 

“Fuck, Jordan…”

Jordan hums like he agrees, sliding one finger back into John’s hole. John cries out again, thighs quivering around Jordan’s head. “Too much.”

“Mm, not for you,” Jordan replies, adding a second finger. “You can take it. You always do.”

He sinks his mouth further down, tongue dragging firm pressure along a vein. John’s head slams back against the pillow. “Jords, I’m – fuck, I’m close, I’m so close.”

Jordan doesn’t slow. He fucks John open again with his fingers, mouth working him in the meanwhile. It’s precise – thrust inside, suck down, curl his fingers, swirl his tongue. 

“Jordan–! I’m– fuck, I’m coming–”

Jordan moans around his cock, and that’s it. John breaks apart with a strangled cry, spilling messily into Jordan’s mouth, onto his tongue, lips, chin. He keeps shaking through it, whimpers spilling out of him in aftershocks. 

Jordan doesn’t let go until John physically pushes at his shoulder. Only then does Jordan lift his head, fingers sliding slowly out of John’s hole. John collapses back into the sheets, chest heaving, glitter stuck to the sweat on his flushed throat. 

Jordan wipes his mouth with the back of his hand, crawls up over him and kisses him slow, letting John taste the remnants of himself on his tongue. Heartbeats thud against sweat-slick skin. John is still trembling faintly, and Jordan cups his jaw.

They’re sticky.

“Alright,” Jordan murmurs, pressing one last slow kiss to John’s temple. “Stay put a sec.”

He eases him off, kissing away the faint pout that appears when John realises they aren’t glued together anymore. Then Jordan slides out of bed with a groan, muscles complaining. He makes his way to the bathroom, running a flannel under warm water, wrings it out until it’s just damp. In the mirror he looks wrecked, but with a stupid soft smile he couldn’t get rid of even if he tried.

When he comes back, John hasn’t moved much. He’s sprawled on his back now, eyes half-lidded.

“Hey,” Jordan says gently, climbing back onto the mattress. “You want to come here for a bit?”

John does. He cleans him up with the cloth, all the sharp edges gone for him. John hisses once when the cloth drags over a sensitive patch of skin, then melts, letting Jordan tip his hips this way and that, press a kiss to his knee, the inside of his wrist.

“You don’t have to,” John mumbles, voice slurred.

“Yeah, I do,” Jordan says, matter-of-fact. “Can’t have you stickin’ to my sheets. With all your cum. And mine.”

John crinkles his eyes. “You’re so gross.”

“Funny, that’s not what you were saying five minutes ago when you were begging–”

“Oh my God,” John throws an arm over his face, but he’s smiling, and Jordan’s smiling.

Jordan chucks the flannel back towards the bathroom and grabs the first clean-ish t-shirt off the chair, coaxing it over John’s head. It’s one of his old ones, soft from a hundred washes. It hangs off John’s shoulders and it always drives Jordan nuts.

“Arms up.”

John obliges, letting Jordan thread him through the sleeves, smooth the hem down over his thighs. His hair’s a mess, fringe stuck to his forehead. Jordan pushes it back gently, carding through it. John actually sighs at that, eyes fluttering close.

“Better?” Jordan asks quietly.

“Mhm,” John hums. “You’re very good at this.”

“Yeah, well, I like lookin’ after you, don’t I?”

The words are true. John opens his eyes at that, looks at him like he’s just been given a second sun. Jordan lies back down beside him, dragging the duvet up over them both. Joh slides automatically beside him, and they lie there in the dim fairy-light morning glow.

It’s peaceful, for a minute. Then John starts fidgeting. He feels it more than he sees it, the way John’s fingers tap lightly against his chest. His head lifts, then settles back down, gaze flicking towards the bedside table than at Jordan. His foot even nudges his calf once, twice, a little rhythm.

“Somethin’ bothering you?” 

John’s head jerks up, eyes too innocent. “No, no. All good.”

“You sure?”

“Yes,” John nods, a little too enthusiastically. “Just, you know. Chillin’.”

He definitely isn’t just chillin’. He’s practically vibrating. Jordan lets it hang for a bit, watching him. John tries to hold his blank, neutral expression for all of three seconds before it eventually cracks.

“Jords,” he says finally. “So… I did my bit, yeah?”

Jordan raises a brow.

John flaps a hand weakly at the fairy lights. “All this. And… y’know. The other stuff. So I was just wondering. Just a little thought. Not a demand, just… is there… you know…” he trails off, cheeks going pink. “A thing.”

“And what thing are we talking about?”

“A Valentine’s thing,” John clarifies. “For me.”

Jordan stares at him. John stares back, wide-eyed and so unbelievably endearing. He’s kicking his foot lightly against Jordan’s ankle. If he had a tail, it would be thumping against the mattress right now. 

Jordan groans eventually. “You’re gonna be the death of me, John Stones, you know that?”

John’s whole face lights up. “Is that a yes? You have something for me?”

“Could’ve at least pretended you weren’t expecting anything,” Jordan grumbles, but he’s already reaching over him to get the drawer. John makes an excited little noise and tries to sit up, only to be pushed gently back down. 

“Stay,” Jordan orders. “Like the good boy you are.”

“Oi, that’s rude,” John mutters, but he’s grinning, knuckles pressed to his mouth to try and hide it. 

Jordan fishes around the drawer, closing around the two things he’s hidden there for days. His stomach does a weird little flip, he suddenly feels stupidly nervous. Which is ridiculous. He’s just had John riding him stupid ten minutes ago, this should not be the part that makes his hands shake. 

John’s gaze drops immediately to Jordan’s hand. “Oh my God! You did wrapping and everything.”

“It’s not wrapping, it’s a bloody sticker.”

Jordan hands John the first thing. It’s a CD in a clear plastic case. There’s a printed tracklist tucked inside, but the front cover is just white paper with blocky handwriting in blue pen.

For Stonesy
Every track is yours x

John’s whole face softens. “Is that–”

“A mixtape,” Jordan says. “Well. Not a tape, obviously. But you know. It’s like a mixtape.”

John touches it like it might vanish, eyes flicking over the messy little hearts and football balls Jordan’s drawn in the corners. “You made me a playlist on an actual CD.”

“One of your cars still plays them,” Jordan says quickly. “And I wanted… I dunno, something personal you could hold. Instead of just links on your phone.”

“What’s on it?”

“Songs that remind me of you,” Jordan says, heat creeping up his neck. “From, like. Different bits. Some from back when we were kids, some from this year. Stuff I put on whenever I miss you. Thought you might like having them all together.”

John smiles at that, but his eyes are shiny now. “Jords…”

“Don’t start crying yet,” Jordan mutters. “There’s more, hang on.”

It’s a small glass jar, about the size of his fist. There’s a ribbon tied around the neck in a bow that’s obviously been redone five times, and stuck to the front is a little label in the same messy blocky writing.

365 reasons i love you
open one a day. DO NOT CHEAT!!!!!!!

Inside, the jar’s crammed full of folded coloured paper, pastel pinks and blues and yellows and greens, all packed in.

“What–what is this?”

Jordan clears his throat, suddenly aware of how childish it looks. “The description’s on the front, genius.”

“No, I mean,” John reaches out, turning it carefully in his hands. “You wrote these? All of them?”

“I did, yeah.”

“All three hundred and–”

“Sixty-five. Yes.”

John tears his gaze away from the jar to stare at Jordan. “When?” 

“Last couple of months,” Jordan shrugs. He watches John’s face closely as he goes on. “I know you’re sick of everyone tellin’ you to keep your head up and be positive, all that bollocks. So I just thought…” He swallows. “Maybe on days when it’s shit, you could open one. Remember that I don’t just love you ‘cause you can kick a ball well.”

John’s lower lip trembles.

“So,” Jordan rushes, trying to fill the thick fragile silence, “there’s all sorts in there. Some of ‘em are dead soppy, like. They’re a reminder. Every day of the year. That even when football’s being a little bitch, you’re still my favourite person.”

He thinks he’s overdone it. John blinks at him, eyes shining. His grip on the jar tightens. “Oh.”

“Don’t cry,” Jordan blurts. “It’s meant to help, not make you–”

“I’m not crying,” John says immediately, except his voice cracks straight through the middle.

“Alright, you’re not crying.”

He leans in and presses his mouth to the soft skin under John’s eye, kissing away the dampness before it can spill over properly. John sucks in a breath, lashes fluttering against his cheek. Jordan moves to the other eye.

“S’all good, pet,” he murmurs. “You don’t have to say anything.”

John nods. “Yeah,” he manages. “Okay. Fuck, Jord, this is–” he breaks off, laughing helplessly. “You wrote me stuff. For everyday.”

He stares down at the jar again, then up at Jordan. “Can I… open one now?”

“No,” Jordan says automatically. “You’re supposed to start tomorrow.”

“Why would you give me a jar full of reasons then tell me I’m not allowed to read any?”

“John.”

“What about if I–”

“No.”

“You didn’t even let me finish.”

“Don’t need to. I know that tone.”

“What if,” he says again, “what if, just because it’s Valentine’s Day and you love me, I open… five.”

“Five?” Jordan repeats, scandalised. “You can’t open five! That’s cheating.”

“Please,” John says, scooting closer, clutching the jar to his chest. “Come on, Jords. You can’t expect me to wait. Just five.”

“That’s not how this works, John.”

“Think of it as a… as a welcome bonus. You know, like when you download a betting app and they give you extra money?”

“You’re comparing my love jar to a betting app?”

John’s mouth twitches. “Love jar,” he repeats, faintly delighted. “Please. Just five. I’ll be really strict after that, I promise. One a day. I’ll even let you monitor me. But now I wanna know what you wrote. I wanna… I wanna see how you see me. For a bit.”

That last bit tickles something in him. He takes a look at him, at the dark smudges under his eyes from restless nights, the faint tension still sitting in his shoulders even now. How he’s clinging to the jar like it’s a life jacket.

He sighs. “Five. And that’s it. You try and sneak in a sixth, I’m takin’ it off and putting a padlock on the lid.”

Jordan’s not sure John even heard him. He opens it very gently, fingers slipping inside to rustle among the little papers until he pinches one out at random. He unfolds it slowly, skimming the handwriting. His mouth curves, whole face lighting up.

“What’s that one say?” Jordan asks. 

“I love you because you always pretend not to like being looked after, even though you melt the second I bring you a cup of tea.” John’s cheeks flush. “That’s not true.”

“It’s extremely true.”

John smiles anyway, folding the note carefully and setting it on the bedside table. “Next one.”

He picks another.

“I love you because you talk to dogs like they’re people and to people like they’re dogs,” John snorts, laughing properly now. “Okay, that’s fair.”

“Go on.”

Third.

“I love you because when you’re injured you act like the world’s ending, but you still cheer louder than anyone else for the lads who are playing.”

John’s smile goes soft at that one. He presses his lips together, looking down at the little scrap in his hand.

“Fourth,” he digs around more carefully this time. Then pulls one out and opens it. “I love you because you fought. Even when it would’ve been easier to give up.”

Jordan watches John. He remembers ugly arguments and slammed doors and weeks of silence that tasted like ash, and the way John still turned up despite everything, stubborn and furious, and said, I don’t want to be anywhere else.

“Last one,” John whispers. “I love you because you never realise how brave you are, and you chose me to remind you every day.”

The tears are back. He doesn’t bother pretending this time. They sit in his lashes, making his blue eyes shine in the fairy-light glow. “Jordan…”

Jordan reaches out, takes the little note from his fingers and adds it to the tiny pile on the bedside table. Then he cups John’s jaw, sweeping away the tear that finally slips free.

“Five,” he says gently. “That’s your lot.”

John nods, sniffling, then suddenly throws himself forward, knocking the jar lid back on with one hand and pressing his face into Jordan’s neck with the other. His voice is muffled when he speaks, choked and overwhelmed all at once. “I love you. I love you so much.”

Jordan wraps both arms around him, the jar in between their hearts. He kisses his hair, his cheeks, his damp eyelashes again for good measure. “Love you too, Johnny. More each day.”

John lets out a shaky little laugh, relaxing fully against him at last. His body fits into Jordan’s like it’s learned the shape by heart.

“You’re gonna regret giving me these, you know.”

“Why’s that?”

“Because,” John mumbles, “every time I forget you’ll have to sit through me reading them out. Over and over again.”

Jordan gives him a quiet laugh. “I think I can live with that.”

He presses his lips to the crown of John’s head, breathing him in, and lets his eyes fall shut.

“Happy Valentine’s, pet,” he whispers once more.
